Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.management

Interface LogBackend

All Known Implementing Classes:
JulLogBackend, Log4j2LogBackend, LogbackLogBackend
public interface LogBackend
SPI behind the /loggers management endpoint: reads and sets logger levels for one concrete logging backend at runtime.

juneau-rest-server ships only the java.util.logging implementation, keeping the core management surface dependency-free. Support for other backends is opt-in: the juneau-rest-server-management-logging add-on ships LogbackLogBackend / Log4j2LogBackend (with the backends declared provided), and any consumer can implement this interface for a backend Juneau does not cover.

Explicit selection (no auto-drive)

Consistent with Juneau's explicit-over-magic stance, the backend is explicitly declared by registering a LoggersSettings bean built with LoggersSettings.Builder.backend(LogBackend). When none is declared, LoggersManager drives JulLogBackend — the endpoint never silently classpath-scans for, and then drives, a backend the operator didn't choose.

Level contract

Levels are exchanged as backend-native level-name strings (e.g. JUL "FINE"/"INFO", Logback / Log4j2 "DEBUG"/"INFO"). Reads report the logger's configured level — the level set on the logger itself, or the empty string when it inherits from an ancestor. Sets are process-lifetime-only (they do not rewrite the backend's configuration file). The root logger is addressed under the key "ROOT".

See Also:
  • JulLogBackend
  • LoggersManager
  • LoggersSettings
  • Management Surface
Since:
10.0.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    String
    getLevel(String name)
    Returns the configured level of a single logger.
    Map<String,String>
    getLevels()
    Returns a snapshot of all known loggers and their configured levels.
    void
    setLevel(String name, String level)
    Sets (or clears) the level of a single logger at runtime (process-lifetime-only).

  • Method Details

    • getLevels

      Map<String,String> getLevels()
      Returns a snapshot of all known loggers and their configured levels.
      Returns:
      A sorted map of logger name → configured level name (empty string = inherited from an ancestor). The root logger is keyed "ROOT". Never null.

    • getLevel

      String getLevel(String name)
      Returns the configured level of a single logger.
      Parameters:
      name - The logger name ("ROOT" or empty for the root logger).
      Returns:
      The configured level name, the empty string if the level is inherited, or null if no such logger is currently registered.

    • setLevel

      void setLevel(String name, String level)
      Sets (or clears) the level of a single logger at runtime (process-lifetime-only).
      Parameters:
      name - The logger name ("ROOT" or empty for the root logger).
      level - The backend-native level name, or null/blank to clear the logger's own level so it inherits from its ancestor.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If level is non-blank but not a valid level name for the backend.
